title: Meatloaf
description: Classic two-loaf meatloaf with a panade of breadcrumbs and half-and-half for a rich, tender texture, finished with a ketchup-brown sugar glaze.

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly grease two loaf pans (8.5x4.5 or 9x5) or line with parchment.
Make the panade: combine breadcrumbs and half-and-half in a large mixing bowl. Mix and let sit 3–5 minutes until fully moistened but not soupy.

Cook the aromatics: heat oil or butter in a small pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook 6–8 minutes until soft and translucent. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Remove from heat and cool 2–3 minutes. This step improves texture and prevents graininess.
Add cooked onion and garlic, eggs, ketchup, Worcestershire, salt, pepper, thyme, and parsley (if using) to the breadcrumb mixture. Mix until evenly combined.
Add ground beef and mix gently with hands just until combined. Do not overmix -- overmixing makes meatloaf dense.
Divide mixture evenly between the two loaf pans. Shape gently -- do not compress tightly. Smooth the tops. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes.
Mix glaze: stir together ketchup, brown sugar, and Worcestershire. Remove meatloaves from oven, spread glaze evenly over each loaf, and return to oven.
Bake 20–30 more minutes until internal temperature reaches 155–160°F. Total cook time 45–55 minutes.
Rest 10–15 minutes before slicing -- essential for clean slices. Cut into 3/4 to 1-inch slices. Each loaf yields about 3 servings.

notes:
Half-and-half produces a richer, more tender result than milk -- use slightly less than you would milk.

Don't compress the meat tightly into the pans and don't overmix -- both make the loaf dense.

Storage: refrigerate up to 4 days. Reheat whole loaf at 300°F for 20–25 minutes, or slice and reheat in a skillet with butter for best texture.
